title: A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or areas
abstract: A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or areas (22), involving the field of natural disaster prevention and control in coastal areas. The fending groyne includes a base (18), a pillar assembly, a baffle (01) and a power device (08). There are several pillar holes (13) arranged on the base (18). The pillar assembly is mounted in the above-mentioned pillar holes (13) of corresponding shape. A conical pillar (14), several convex pillars (02) and above-mentioned baffle (01) installed between the protrusions of every two convex pillars (02) as mentioned together form an inverted V-shaped pillar assembly. The terminal pillar (17) is arranged at the end of the above-mentioned inverted V-shaped pillar assembly. The fending groyne can change the direction of high winds and large waves and gradually reduce the height or intensity of the wave from the upper layer to the lower layer, so as to protect important facilities or areas (22) along and near the coast. The fending groyne is strong in stability and the base (18) can reduce damage to the fending groyne caused by earthquakes.
claims:
Claims ( 7 ) A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or 
areas is characterized as follows: The fending groyne mentioned comprises a 
base (18), a pillar assembly, a baffle (01) and a power device (08); the base 
(18) mentioned is an integral fixed plate and is fixed in front of the 
protected important facilities or areas through the foundation (16); there are 
several pillar holes (13) arranged on the base (18), the pillar assembly 
mentioned includes several convex pillars (02), conical pillars (14) and 
terminal pillars (17); the pillar assembly is installed in the pillar holes 
(13) mentioned of corresponding shape and the conical pillar (14) mentioned is 
located in the center of the fending groyne. There are concession grooves 
provided on the two conical surfaces of the conical pillar (14) mentioned, 
while baffles (01) are included in the concession grooves mentioned extending 
towards both sides. The baffles (01) extending towards both sides connect the 
protrusions (03) of the adjacent convex pillars (02) mentioned and form an 
inverted V-shaped pillar assembly together with the mentioned baffles (01) 
hoisted between the protrusions (03) of every two convex pillars (02) and the 
protrusions (03) of the multiple convex pillars (02) mentioned. The terminal 
pillar (17) mentioned is arranged at the end of the above-mentioned inverted 
V-shaped pillar assembly; the baffle (01) mentioned is made up of several 
folding plates (05) formed by the hinge assembly (07); the power device (08) 
mentioned is located where every two folding plates (05) are connected. A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or 
areas as mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized as follows: The hinge assembly 
(07) mentioned includes a pivot as well as gears mounted on the pivot, the 
power device (08) mentioned includes an electric motor as well as gears 
installed at the output end of the motor. Additionally, the gear at the output 
end of the motor mentioned engages with the gear on the pivot of the hinge 
assembly (07) mentioned, while the power device (08) mentioned drives the pivot 
of the hinge assembly (07) mentioned to rotate, thereby folding or unfolding 
the mentioned baffle (01). A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or 
areas as mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized as follows: The above-mentioned 
pillar holes (13) on the base (18) mentioned include convex pillar holes, 
conical pillar holes and terminal pillar holes; the above-mentioned convex 
pillars (02), conical pillars (14) and terminal pillars (17) are installed in 
the corresponding pillar holes (13), respectively. A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or 
areas as mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized as follows: The above-mentioned 
fending groyne comprises multi-level fending groynes arranged in a parallel 
manner and the upper part of the above-mentioned pillar assembly for each level 
of the fending groyne is connected by several connecting beams (19); the 
above-mentioned connecting beams (19) are arranged between the front and rear 
corresponding pillars; the protrusions (03) on the front end of the convex 
pillars (02) and the terminal pillars (17) directly face incoming waves and the 
connecting beams (19) of the convex pillars (02) and the terminal pillars (17) 
are vertically connected to the front of the protrusion (03) of the convex 
pillars (02) and the terminal pillars (17), respectively, for the latter 
fending groyne. The connecting beam (19) of the conical pillar (14) is 
connected to the whole front of the conical pillar (14) of the latter fending 
groyne. A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or 
areas as mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized as follows: There are winches 
(06) provided on the tops of the convex pillar (02), the conical pillar (14) 
and the terminal pillar (17), respectively, and the above-mentioned wrenches 
(06) are connected to the top of the folding plate (05) at the topmost part of 
the baffle (01) by means of the pulling rope (09). Grooves (15) are arranged at 
the upper end of the convex pillar (02), the conical pillar (14) and the 
terminal pillar (17) to reduce the wear of the pulling rope (09). A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or 
areas as mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized as follows: There are ladder 
reinforcement bars (04) provided at the rear of the above-mentioned convex 
pillar (02), the conical pillar (14) and the terminal pillar (17), 
respectively. A fending groyne for protecting important coastal facilities or 
areas as mentioned in Claim 1 is characterized as follows: There are fender 
courses provided at the outside or in the middle of the above-mentioned baffle 
(01) and at the outside of the convex pillar (02), the conical pillar (14) and 
the terminal pillar (17).
